name: php_fpm
explanation: PHP-FPM exposes status/ping over an HTTP path that the user must configure on a FastCGI passthrough (typically nginx or Apache). The path is conventionally /status and /ping but is fully user-configurable in the FPM pool config (pm.status_path, ping.path) and depends on the front-end web server's routing. The check requires that at least one of status_url or ping_url be supplied; both default to localhost examples but no port/path is universal. Direct FastCGI mode (use_fastcgi=true) targets port 9000 directly but still needs the path. Discovery is feasible only via probing several plausible paths (/status, /ping, /fpm-status, /fpm-ping) and possibly direct FastCGI on 9000.
notes: No auto_conf.yaml ships with the integration. Pattern G (multiple plausible URL paths). Direct FastCGI mode could be probed at port 9000, but conventional deployments rely on the web server proxying status to a non-default path.
